standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per positive state episode

approved_boundary_notice.reason: Amsterdam processitems exposes explicit start/stop intervals for itemid 12465 / CVVH, which is narrow enough for CRRT-family active support when hemodialysis, dialysis-line access, peritoneal catheter, plasma exchange, CVVH orders, device parameters, medication protocol rows, and fluid-removal measurements remain outside the same-name output.
approved_boundary_notice.accepted_policy_scope: conservative CVVH positive episode boundary for CRRT-family active support

source_filter_lock.required_interval_rule: start, stop, and duration are non-null and positive; stop must be greater than start

database_mapping.source_grain: one Amsterdam processitems CVVH interval row before CRRT-family interval union
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained positive CRRT-family support state episode row after merging overlapping approved CVVH intervals within the stay

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: patientid joined from Amsterdam admissions_core by admissionid
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: not separately available in the current Amsterdam opening mapping
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: raw admissionid is the Amsterdam ICU/MC local admission identifier and normalizes to stay_id under ICU/MC semantics

excluded_statuses: 16352 Peritoneaal catheter peritoneal access catheter, not active dialysis interval
procedureorderitems CVVH orders such as start, stop, reset, filter change, and lab collection
drugitems CVVH protocol medication or fluid rows
numericitems CVVH device parameters, settings, pressures, MFT rows, and fluid-removal volumes
adjacent_status_boundary.boundary_reason: This same-name variable represents Amsterdam CVVH-derived CRRT-family active support, not umbrella any-RRT, hemodialysis, exact modality truth, access-line presence, peritoneal catheter presence, fluid-removal events, orders, device parameters, or renal phenotypes.

state_episode_build_translation.positive_state_translation: retain Amsterdam processitems intervals for itemid 12465 CVVH and merge overlapping or exactly contiguous retained CVVH intervals within the same local admission
state_episode_build_translation.episode_start_translation: use merged source start as Amsterdam relative support_starttime in milliseconds while also emitting relative_start_time_value in minutes
state_episode_build_translation.episode_end_translation: use merged source stop as Amsterdam relative support_endtime in milliseconds while also emitting relative_end_time_value in minutes
state_episode_build_translation.continuity_translation: merge overlapping or exactly contiguous approved CVVH processitems intervals; any positive gap starts a new support episode
state_episode_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained row means no positive CRRT-family episode under this processitems source rule

validation_contract.positive_only_no_row_policy_check: false rows are not emitted; absence of row is not universal proof of no CRRT-family support or no RRT
validation_contract.source_code_lock_rule: all retained rows must originate from Amsterdam processitems itemid 12465 CVVH
notes: This Amsterdam mapping is reviewed-approved for std_crrt_family_active.
The approved source scope is processitems active intervals for CVVH.
Hemodialyse, access-line rows, peritoneal catheter rows, plasma exchange, orders, device parameters, medication protocol rows, and fluid-removal rows remain outside this same-name output.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: Formal Amsterdam same-name output is approved only through the governed execution.py path reading this approved mapping spec and producing runtime plus rerun evidence.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading an approved mapping spec and producing runtime plus rerun evidence.
